area: MSC 06A07, 03F15, 03B70, 68Q42 — well-quasi-orders / termination orders, ordinal analysis, reverse mathematics, string rewriting
hole_justification: Searches run: (1) "Yolcu Aaronson Heule Collatz rewriting matrix interpretations" — found the one serious paper (arXiv:2105.14697, CADE'21 + JAR 2023); downloaded and grepped its FULL TEXT: zero occurrences of 'simplification', 'simply terminating', 'self-embedding', 'path order', 'Kruskal', 'Higman' — they never address simplification orders. (2) Downloaded Zantema's original TORPA paper (JAR 2005) and read Section 10 in full: he proves SN(C) iff Collatz (Thm 16) and says only 'As expected, TORPA is not able to prove termination of C' — no simple-termination remark, despite his own derivation bh1^(2n+1)b →* bh1^(3n+2)b being self-embedding at n=1. (3) 'Zantema Collatz "simply terminating" OR "simplification order"', '"Collatz" "well-quasi-order" OR "ordinal analysis" OR "Kruskal"' — nothing. So (a) is TRUE, one-line provable, and unstated in print as far as I can find. (4) '"reverse mathematics" Collatz proof-theoretic strength', 'Collatz "bounded arithmetic" OR "open induction" OR "IΔ0" OR "IΣ1"', MathOverflow queries — nothing serious on (b); no proof-theoretic analysis of Terras 1976 exists; only non-peer-reviewed claims (Feinstein math/0312309; Rahn–Sultanow–Aberkane arXiv:2101.09719, math.GM, claims a full proof via 'Hydra games' — not a real hydra/ordinal connection). (5) 'Weiermann "phase transition" unprovability Collatz' — confirmed zero connection; the phase-transition program has never touched Collatz (c). (6) 'arctic ... Collatz Zantema impossibility 2023..2026' — no follow-up has closed YAH's explicitly-stated open problem (arctic no-go for Z, and any no-go for their mixed-base system T). What DOES exist nearby: reverse math of termination METHODS (Steila–Yokoyama on Podelski–Rybalchenko; Frittaion–Pelupessy–Steila–Yokoyama: SCT soundness = WO(ω^ω^ω) over RCA₀) never applied to Collatz; and Touzet's CSL'97 proof-theoretic calibration of a different single self-embedding SRS (Zantema's problem 0011→111000, PRA-conservative proof) — the exact methodological template, never applied to the Collatz system. Verdict: one serious paper on the bridge (YAH) plus its source (Zantema 2005); everything the task asks about in (a),(b),(c) is undone.
bridge: Objects: Collatz T-orbits on N ↔ derivations of Zantema's 7-rule SRS Z over {1,b,h,s,t} (bh1^n b encodes n; bh1^(2n)b →* bh1^n b and bh1^(2n+1)b →* bh1^(3n+2)b; SN(Z) ⟺ Collatz, Zantema 2005 Thm 16). Maps: a termination proof of Z = a compatible well-founded monotone algebra/reduction order; termination technology is stratified along TWO calibrated axes: (i) certificate order-strength — simplification orders (Higman/Kruskal wqo; RM strength up to WO(ω^ω^ω) for Higman, ϑ(Ω^ω) for Kruskal) < matrix interpretations (order type ~ω^d lex on N^d) < ordinal interpretations (ZWM14) < size-change/transition invariants (WO(ω^ω^ω), weak-Ramsey — FPSY, Steila–Yokoyama); (ii) derivational-complexity ceiling forced by the certificate — match-bounds & single-step arctic: LINEAR dc (GHW04, KW09); single-step natural matrix: exponential (EWZ08); MPO: primitive recursive (Hofbauer 92); LPO: multiply recursive (Weiermann 95); KBO: Ackermannian (Lepper 01). What corresponds to what: a Collatz proof ↔ a point in this (ordinal strength × dc ceiling) landscape; structural features of Collatz dynamics ↔ exclusion theorems: the 2-adic cascade n=2^k−1 rising k steps gives BOTH self-embedding derivations (bh1^3b →+ bh1^5b, killing all simplification orders via Dershowitz 1982) AND an unconditional dc lower bound dc_Z(ℓ) = Ω(ℓ^(log₂3)) ≈ ℓ^1.585 (verified numerically: ratio → 2.000·ℓ^1.585), killing every linear-dc method; YAH's Berstel-pumping on the 3-step family 8n+1 ↦ 9n+2 kills natural matrix interpretations of every dimension.
strongest_adjacent_theorems: Yolcu–Aaronson–Heule (CADE'21/JAR 2023, arXiv:2105.14697) Thms 3.8 & 3.10: no natural (ℕ-semiring) matrix interpretation of ANY dimension can remove even one rule of Zantema's Z, even after dependency-pair transformation; proof via Berstel's theorem on ℕ-rational sequences applied to f(8n+1)>f(9n+2). Arctic case and mixed-base system T explicitly left open.

Dershowitz (TCS 1982): a self-embedding rewrite system admits no termination proof by any simplification order (via Higman/Kruskal); consequences: RPO/LPO/MPO/KBO and all strictly monotone 1-dim ℕ-interpretations (incl. polynomial) fail on self-embedding systems.
Frittaion–Pelupessy–Steila–Yokoyama (arXiv:1709.09036): soundness of size-change termination (ISCT) is equivalent to WO(ω^ω^ω) over RCA₀ — the model 'reverse math of a termination method' theorem.

Steila–Yokoyama (arXiv:1512.08622, APAL): Podelski–Rybalchenko Termination Theorem is strictly weaker than RT²₂ over RCA₀ (answers Gasarch's Open Problems 2 & 3): no program can witness that transition-invariant termination needs full Ramsey for pairs.

Derivational-complexity calibration ladder: Hofbauer–Lautemann 1989 (polynomial interpretations ⇒ double-exp dc); Hofbauer 1992 (MPO ⇒ primitive recursive); Weiermann 1995 (LPO ⇒ multiply recursive); Lepper 2001 (KBO ⇒ Ackermannian); Geser–Hofbauer–Waldmann 2004 (match-bounded SRS ⇒ linear dc); Endrullis–Waldmann–Zantema 2008 (natural matrix ⇒ exponential); Koprowski–Waldmann 2009 (arctic ⇒ linear).

Rathjen–Weiermann 1993: proof-theoretic ordinal of Kruskal's theorem is ϑ(Ω^ω) (small Veblen); Weiermann's phase-transition program locates exact provability thresholds for miniaturized wqo statements — the technology the task asks to transplant.

Touzet (CSL 1997): proof-theoretic calibration of one specific self-embedding one-rule SRS (Zantema's problem 0011→111000): termination provable in a theory conservative over PRA, despite escaping all simplification orders — proof that 'escapes simplification orders' does NOT imply high logical strength.
the_push: Pushing the analogy through gives a genuine program, but with one brutal honest twist. The twist: ALL existing ordinal-calibrated unprovability (hydra, Goodstein, Kruskal miniaturizations, Weiermann thresholds) detects FAST GROWTH of witness functions; Collatz's witness (stopping time) is conjecturally ~log n, and dc_Z is conjecturally polynomial (~ℓ^2), so growth-rate technology is STRUCTURALLY INAPPLICABLE — a Weiermann-style 'Collatz needs ordinal α' theorem cannot come from derivation lengths, and there is no cheap route to PA-independence (a true Π⁰₂ sentence with slow witnesses can still be independent, but proving that needs diagonalization-type tools nobody has for Collatz). What survives, and is real: a two-axis BARRIER MAP with each exclusion provable. Rung 1 (new-in-print, one line): Z is self-embedding (bh1^3b →+ bh1^5b), so ALL simplification orders — the entire wqo/Higman/Kruskal-based stratum, of any proof-theoretic strength — fail; this is the precise answer to (a): the failure is structural (Collatz excursions), orthogonal to ordinal strength, exactly as Touzet's PRA-conservative Zantema-problem analysis warns. Rung 2 (new, unconditional): dc_Z(ℓ) = Ω(ℓ^(log₂3)) via the 2-adic cascade n=2^k−1, so every linear-dc-certifying method (match-bounds, single-step arctic) provably fails. Rung 3 = YAH's matrix no-go. Rung 4 (open, attackable): arctic/max-plus no-go via ultimate periodicity of one-letter max-plus rational series (cyclicity theorem) replacing Berstel — closes YAH's stated open problem. The emergent insight, unifying with our ≤7-state DFA-certificate exhaustion and Monks 2006: every excluded certificate class is (max,+)/ℕ-RATIONAL or automatic — the conjectured meta-barrier is a 'regularity barrier': no certificate whose semantics is a rational/automatic sequence family can witness Collatz convergence, because Collatz's self-embedding family 8n+1 ↦ 9n+2 pumps against rationality. Each instance is rigorously provable; the map tells us a real proof must use non-rational invariants (2-adic valuation, entropy/measure — where Tao 2019 actually lives). That is the rigorous version of 'why all simple arguments fail' — as certificate-class lower bounds, not theory-strength lower bounds.
first_move: Prove the arctic analogue of YAH Theorem 3.8: 'no arctic (max-plus) matrix interpretation of any dimension removes any rule of Z.' Skeleton: f(k) = v ⊗ M^⊗k ⊗ w (one-letter max-plus rational series) is eventually arithmetic-periodic — f(k+c) = f(k) + λ_j·c on each residue class j mod c (max-plus cyclicity/Kleene–Schützenberger, Gaubert; Cohen–Dubois–Quadrat–Viot) — then f(8n+1) > f(9n+2) for all n forces slope inequalities 8λ ≥ 9λ' across classes that pigeonhole to a contradiction, exactly as Berstel's theorem did in the ℕ case. Estimated: 2–4 weeks; closes the open problem YAH explicitly pose in their conclusion. Package with the two free rungs: (i) the self-embedding derivation bh1^3b →+_Z bh1^5b ⟹ no simplification order (formalize in Lean in a weekend: finite derivation + Higman-embedding check); (ii) dc_Z(ℓ) = Ω(ℓ^(log₂3)) (verified numerically above, unconditional) ⟹ no match-bounds / single-step arctic. Parallel computation on the 32-core machines: SAT-search (their tool or Matchbox/TTT2) for natural+arctic interpretations of the mixed-base system T at dimensions 1–6 to locate empirically where T's obstruction begins — YAH's pumping argument provably does NOT extend to T (they say so), so T is where the next theorem or the next surprise lives.
payoff_odds: Realistic best case: a publishable 'termination lower-bound ladder for Collatz' (JAR/FSCD/LMCS level) — simplification orders (all ordinals), linear-dc methods, natural AND arctic matrix interpretations of all dimensions — closing YAH's open problem, plus a unified 'regularity barrier' meta-theorem merging with our DFA-exhaustion program; genuine guidance that any proof needs non-rational (2-adic/measure-theoretic) invariants. Likely case: rungs 1–2 certain (~99%, trivial but new in print); arctic no-go ~70% (max-plus periodicity has reducible-matrix corner cases; slopes-per-residue-class bookkeeping is fiddly but the 8n+1/9n+2 family hits all residues); a no-go for the mixed-base system T is the real prize and genuinely hard (~15% — rationality is lost, which is exactly why they built T). Honest negatives: this program CANNOT produce an ordinal/reverse-math lower bound on the theory needed for Collatz — question (b)'s dream — because all known strength-lower-bound technology runs through witness growth, and Collatz's witnesses are conjecturally logarithmic; Terras 1976 is EFA-formalizable counting and its reverse-math analysis would land at the bottom, worth a page not a paper. Probability any of this proves Collatz: <2%. Value is barrier cartography: rigorous, cumulative, and it retires whole certificate classes our program would otherwise waste compute searching.

prior_art.ref: Zantema 2005, 'Termination of string rewriting proved automatically', J. Automated Reasoning 34:105–139, §10 Thm 16
prior_art.what: Origin of the bridge: 7-rule/5-symbol SRS C (=Z), SN(C) ⟺ Collatz; correctness proof itself uses semantic labelling; notes only that TORPA fails on C — no simple-termination or proof-theoretic remark.

THE serious paper: mixed binary–ternary system T with SN(T) ⟺ Collatz; Thms 3.8/3.10: no natural matrix interpretation of any dimension removes any rule of Z (even with dependency pairs), via Berstel's theorem on ℕ-rational sequences and the 8n+1 →* 9n+2 family; automated proofs of weakenings (Farkas variant); explicitly leaves open the arctic no-go and any no-go for T. Never mentions simplification orders/self-embedding/wqo.

prior_art.ref: Touzet, 'A proof-theoretical investigation of Zantema's problem', CSL 1997, LNCS 1414
prior_art.what: Methodological template for a DIFFERENT system: the self-embedding one-rule SRS 0011→111000; extracts a termination proof in a theory conservative over PRA — shows escaping simplification orders coexists with low logical strength. Never applied to the Collatz SRS.

prior_art.ref: Frittaion–Pelupessy–Steila–Yokoyama, 'The strength of SCT soundness', J. Logic & Computation / arXiv:1709.09036
prior_art.what: Reverse math of a termination method: SCT soundness ⟺ WO(ω^ω^ω) over RCA₀. Collatz never mentioned.

prior_art.ref: Steila–Yokoyama, 'Reverse mathematical bounds for the Termination Theorem', APAL 167(12) 2016, arXiv:1512.08622
prior_art.what: Podelski–Rybalchenko Termination Theorem calibrated below RT²₂ over RCA₀; answers Gasarch's Open Problems 2–3. Collatz never mentioned.

prior_art.ref: Geser–Hofbauer–Waldmann 2004; Endrullis–Waldmann–Zantema 2008; Koprowski–Waldmann 2009; Hofbauer 1992; Weiermann 1995; Lepper 2001; Hofbauer–Lautemann 1989
prior_art.what: The derivational-complexity calibration ladder (match-bounds/arctic: linear; natural matrix: exponential; MPO: prim.rec.; LPO: mult.rec.; KBO: Ackermann; polynomial: 2-exp) — the 'ordinal calibration of termination technology'; never confronted with the Collatz SRS's unconditional dc lower bound.

prior_art.ref: Zankl–Winkler–Middeldorp, 'Beyond polynomials and Peano arithmetic — automation of elementary and ordinal interpretations', JSC 2014
prior_art.what: Automation of ordinal interpretations (above ε₀-style orders) in termination provers — the natural next certificate class to test against Z/T; no Collatz contact.

NON-peer-reviewed: 'Collatz convergence is a Hydra game' claims a full ZFC proof (math.GM, community-rejected); Feinstein claims unprovability via an information-theoretic heuristic. Neither is real prior art for (b)/(c); flagged UNRELIABLE in filename.

prior_art.ref: De Mol 2008 (2-tag system); Kurtz–Simon 2007; Endrullis–Geuvers–Zantema, 'Degrees of undecidability in term rewriting', CSL 2009
prior_art.what: Context: Collatz as halting of a 3-rule 2-tag system (another unary encoding, same self-embedding growth); Π⁰₂-completeness of generalized Collatz and of uniform termination of rewriting — matching upper bounds for the barrier map.
